tasks.title: Backend: extend attachment schema with optional author field
tasks.description: In src/handlers/tasks.js: (1) add VALID_ATTACHMENT_AUTHORS constant ['user','agent']; (2) extend validateAttachments() to accept and validate an optional author field — if present it must be 'user' or 'agent', if absent it is preserved as undefined; (3) extend stripAttachmentContent() to include author in the stripped response (it is metadata, not sensitive content); (4) ensure the normalized data object returned by validateAttachments() carries author.
acceptance_criteria: PATCH /attachments with { name, type, content, author:'user' } returns 200 and the attachment includes author:'user' in the stored task
PATCH /attachments without author field returns 200 and the stored attachment has no author field (undefined passthrough, not null)
PATCH /attachments with author:'invalid' returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR
GET /tasks/:id returns attachments with author field included when present (not stripped)
Existing tests still pass (no regression on attachment validation or strip)

tasks.title: Backend: add DELETE /tasks/:taskId/attachments/:name endpoint
tasks.description: In src/handlers/tasks.js: (1) add TASK_ATTACHMENT_BY_NAME_ROUTE regex /^\/tasks\/([^/]+)\/attachments\/([^/]+)$/ (must come before TASK_SINGLE_ROUTE in the router); (2) add handleDeleteAttachment(req, res, taskId, encodedName) that: URL-decodes the name, looks up the task (404 if missing), finds the attachment by name (404 NOT_FOUND if absent), rejects with 403 FORBIDDEN if attachment.author is not 'user', removes the attachment from the array, calls store.updateTask, returns 200 { task: updatedTask } (stripped); (3) emit a structured log event { event: 'attachments.user_delete', spaceId, taskId, name } to process.stderr; (4) add router dispatch: when method==='DELETE' and TASK_ATTACHMENT_BY_NAME_ROUTE matches, call handleDeleteAttachment.
acceptance_criteria: DELETE /spaces/:spaceId/tasks/:taskId/attachments/My%20Link returns 200 with the attachment removed
DELETE on a name that doesn't exist returns 404 NOT_FOUND
DELETE on a task that doesn't exist returns 404 TASK_NOT_FOUND
DELETE on an attachment with no author field returns 403 FORBIDDEN
DELETE on an attachment with author:'agent' returns 403 FORBIDDEN
DELETE on an attachment with author:'user' returns 200
Attachment name with spaces and special chars is correctly URL-decoded
After deletion, GET /tasks/:taskId confirms attachment is gone

tasks.title: Frontend: extend types and API client
tasks.description: Two changes: (1) In frontend/src/types/index.ts, add author?: 'user' | 'agent' to the Attachment interface. (2) In frontend/src/api/client.ts, add two new exports: patchUserAttachment(spaceId, taskId, attachment) that calls PATCH /spaces/:spaceId/tasks/:taskId/attachments with body { attachments: [attachment] } and returns Promise<Task>; and deleteUserAttachment(spaceId, taskId, name) that calls DELETE /spaces/:spaceId/tasks/:taskId/attachments/:encodedName and returns Promise<Task>. Both functions use the existing apiFetch helper.
acceptance_criteria: patchUserAttachment is exported from api/client.ts and accepted by TypeScript with the correct signature
deleteUserAttachment is exported from api/client.ts and accepted by TypeScript with the correct signature
Attachment interface compiles with author?: 'user' | 'agent' added
No changes to UpdateTaskPayload (attachments go through sub-route, not task PUT)

tasks.title: Frontend: add addUserAttachment and deleteUserAttachment store actions
tasks.description: In frontend/src/stores/useAppStore.ts: (1) Define NewUserAttachment interface { name, type, content } — author:'user' is injected by the action. (2) Add addUserAttachment(taskId, attachment) action: injects author:'user', applies an optimistic update that appends the new attachment to detailTask.attachments and to the matching task in the board, calls patchUserAttachment, reconciles with server response on success, or rolls back + showToast on error. (3) Add deleteUserAttachment(taskId, name) action: applies an optimistic update that removes the named attachment, calls deleteUserAttachment API, reconciles on success, rolls back + showToast on error. Both actions set/clear isMutating. Export types in the store interface.
acceptance_criteria: addUserAttachment results in the new attachment appearing in detailTask.attachments with author:'user'
deleteUserAttachment results in the attachment disappearing from detailTask.attachments
Optimistic update is applied before the API call resolves
On API error, the board and detailTask revert to pre-action state
isMutating is true during the API call and false after
showToast is called with success or error message
TypeScript: store interface compiles without errors

tasks.title: Frontend: new AddAttachmentForm component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/board/AddAttachmentForm.tsx. Props: { spaceId, taskId, existingNames: string[], disabled: boolean, onSuccess: () => void, onCancel: () => void }. Internal state: type ('link'|'text'|'file', default 'link'), name (string), content (string), error (string|null), saving (boolean). Layout: (1) Segmented type selector row: three buttons [Link] [Note] [File Path] — active type uses primary/15 bg. (2) Name field: text input, auto-populates from URL hostname on content blur when name is still empty and type='link'. (3) Content field: URL input for 'link', textarea for 'text', path input for 'file' — label changes accordingly. (4) Error row: shown only when error is set. (5) Footer: [Cancel] (ghost) + [Add] (primary) buttons. Client-side validation: type='link' must be valid https:// URL; type='file' must start with '/'; name must be non-empty, ≤100 chars, not in existingNames. On submit: call useAppStore addUserAttachment, then call onSuccess(). Use existing Button, design tokens (no inline styles). Use Material Symbols icons: 'link', 'notes', 'folder_open' for the type selector.
acceptance_criteria: Renders all three type options as a segmented selector; active type is visually highlighted
Submitting a valid link attachment calls addUserAttachment and then onSuccess()
Submitting a valid text note calls addUserAttachment and then onSuccess()
Submitting a valid file path calls addUserAttachment and then onSuccess()
Submitting with a name that already exists (in existingNames) shows a name-conflict error, no API call
Submitting a non-https URL shows a validation error, no API call
Submitting a file path not starting with '/' shows a validation error, no API call
Name auto-populates from URL hostname on blur when name is empty and type='link'
Cancel button calls onCancel() without making any API call
disabled prop makes both type selector and all inputs non-interactive
No inline styles; Tailwind tokens only
TypeScript: no errors

tasks.title: Frontend: refactor TaskDetailPanel attachments section
tasks.description: In frontend/src/components/board/TaskDetailPanel.tsx: (1) Make the attachments section always render (remove the guard `detailTask.attachments && detailTask.attachments.length > 0`). (2) Add header row: 'Attachments' label + a '+' icon button that toggles the AddAttachmentForm inline below the list. (3) When showAddForm is true, render <AddAttachmentForm> passing existingNames from detailTask.attachments and the store actions. (4) Empty state: when attachments.length === 0 and showAddForm is false, render a small 'No attachments' placeholder. (5) Agent attachment row (author is undefined or 'agent'): keep current visual, no delete button, icon is type-based. (6) User attachment row (author === 'user'): bg-primary/[0.04] background, fixed icon 'person', small 'You' text badge (text-primary/50, text-[10px]), delete button (×) that is visible on hover — calls deleteUserAttachment store action; no confirmation dialog. (7) Wire up the + button disabled state to isReadOnly. (8) The section sits in the right sidebar between Pipeline and the footer.
acceptance_criteria: Attachments section renders even when task has 0 attachments
Empty state text 'No attachments' is shown when there are 0 attachments and the form is not open
Clicking the + button shows AddAttachmentForm; clicking it again (or Cancel) hides it
After successful add, AddAttachmentForm collapses and the new attachment appears in the list
User attachment row has a different background from agent rows
User attachment row shows the 'You' badge
User attachment row shows a delete X button on hover
Clicking delete X removes the attachment (optimistic) and shows a toast
Agent attachment row has no delete button
isReadOnly disables the + button and AddAttachmentForm
Link-type user attachments still open in a new tab on click
Non-link user attachments still open AttachmentModal / MarkdownModal on click

tasks.title: Backend tests: author field + DELETE endpoint
tasks.description: In tests/ (Node.js test runner): (1) Add test cases to the existing attachment test file covering: validateAttachments with author:'user' passes; validateAttachments with author:'agent' passes; validateAttachments with author:'invalid' fails with VALIDATION_ERROR; author field is present in GET /tasks/:id when stored; stripAttachmentContent passes author through. (2) New test suite for DELETE /spaces/:spaceId/tasks/:taskId/attachments/:name covering all acceptance criteria from T-002. Use the in-memory store pattern already used by existing tests.
acceptance_criteria: All new test cases pass via npm test
Coverage of DELETE endpoint: happy path, 404 task, 404 attachment, 403 agent-owned
Coverage of author field validation: user, agent, invalid, missing
No regressions in existing test suite

tasks.title: Frontend tests: AddAttachmentForm + TaskDetailPanel integration
tasks.description: In frontend/src/components/board/__tests__/: (1) AddAttachmentForm.test.tsx: unit tests for validation logic, auto-populate hostname, submit calls addUserAttachment, cancel calls onCancel, name conflict shows error. Mock useAppStore. (2) Add to existing TaskDetailPanel tests (or create a new file): attachments section always renders; empty state; user vs agent row rendering; delete button visible only on user rows; + button toggles form. Use React Testing Library + Vitest.
acceptance_criteria: All new tests pass via cd frontend && npm test
AddAttachmentForm: validates URL, path, name uniqueness before submit
AddAttachmentForm: submits with author:'user' injected
TaskDetailPanel: renders empty state for 0 attachments
TaskDetailPanel: delete button present on user row, absent on agent row
No regressions in existing frontend tests
